steps.title: Identify the key relationship
steps.content: Chile is a proper noun that directly specifies which member/nation the sentence is referring to. This is essential information, not a side comment or optional detail.

steps.title: Match the punctuation to the relationship
steps.content: When a proper noun directly specifies the preceding noun as essential information, Standard English uses no punctuation between them. “Nation Chile” correctly omits punctuation. The other choices incorrectly add punctuation (comma, colon, em dash) that would signal extra or parenthetical information, which isn't appropriate when the proper noun is the essential identifier. That matches choice A — A is correct.
